title: Flagging stale decisions
question: Should the viewer flag decisions that may be stale and due for review?
rationale: Add an optional 'reviewBy' date: most decisions set none and stay calm, but a provisional one ('revisit later') can carry a date the viewer flags when it passes. Opt-in serves those few at no cost to the rest, and d15's supersede path still covers staleness you've already noticed.

options.label: Optional review-by date
options.note: An optional 'reviewBy' field, in the grammar of built (d43) / version (d36): invisible until set, so it only ever applies to decisions you deliberately mark as provisional.

tradeoffs.note: flags only decisions you marked, exactly when their date passes — no false alarms
invisible by default; nothing shown for the decisions you don't mark
a field to set, but only on the few provisional decisions — not every one (the o14b cost, scoped down)
needs new viewer support to surface the flag when a date is due
derived from the date already stored; nothing new to capture
age isn't staleness; a 2-year-old decision can be perfectly current
precise and intentional — flags only what you marked
a new field to set on every decision; easy to forget
no nagging; rely on the human re-reading
the thing a review tool should help with goes unaided
